title: sorbus americana subsp. microcarpa
intro: Sorbus americana subsp. microcarpa, also known as American mountain ash, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sorbus americana subsp. microcarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
